Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Table 2 lists line 1F3-9 with Pt Gene ID Potri.016G086400 and gene name IRREGULAR XYLEM 9 (IRX9).

Source_Species.reason: The FLcDNA library was made from developing xylem of an 'aspen tree' identified in Methods as Populus tremuloides, not P. tremula × P. alba.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Source tissue for the library is described as aspen/Populus tremuloides, so P. tremula × P. alba is not supported as the source species.

Applied_Species.reason: The FOX library was transformed into poplar; the study repeatedly refers to transgenic poplar lines.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods state transformation was into Populus tremula × Populus alba wild type.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Discussion explicitly states IRX9 is 'involved in xylan biosynthesis'; hemicellulose/xylan is consistent with the trait context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Xylan/hemicellulose is a polysaccharide cell wall component; this is directly inferable from the paper's cell wall chemistry context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper frames cellulose/hemicellulose/lignin as major cell wall constituents required for wood formation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The study focuses on developing xylem and secondary cell wall composition in wood; xylan/hemicellulose accumulation in xylem secondary wall is directly implied.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper says IRX9 is a 'non-CLS gene involved in xylan biosynthesis' but does not describe it as a glycosyltransferase-related protein.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure 3 shows most recovered homologs have highest native expression in xylem, but the paper does not explicitly single out IRX9 in this sentence.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: IRX9 FLcDNA was recovered from FOX line 1F3-9, and the inserted FLcDNAs are described as highly overexpressed in FOX lines under the 35S promoter.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods describe the FOX binary vector using the CaMV 35S promoter for FLcDNA overexpression.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Table 1 shows line 1F3-9 had significant MC% and lignin changes, but no direct assay links the IRX9 insert causally to these traits in Results.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The line was identified through the FOX overexpression screen, insert recovery/sequencing, expression analysis of inserted FLcDNAs, and trait profiling.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Screening was performed under greenhouse conditions.

Omics_Data.reason: Results explicitly cite published RNA-seq data from Shi et al. 2017 for native expression analysis; no accession is provided.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a plausible implication, but the paper does not explicitly present IRX9 as a breeding/engineering target.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion states several mutants carried FLcDNAs homologous to known cell wall genes including IRX9, and no recapitulation was reported for IRX9.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point matches paper content: IRX9-homolog recovered in FOX line 1F3-9, IRX9 discussed as involved in xylan biosynthesis, and recovery supports enrichment of wood cell-wall genes.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Table 2 lists line 3F90-4 with Pt Gene ID Potri.008G012400 and gene name FLA17.

Source_Species.reason: Library source was an aspen tree/Populus tremuloides, not explicitly P. tremula × P. alba.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: P. tremula × P. alba is the transformation recipient, whereas the cDNA library source is described as aspen/Populus tremuloides.

Applied_Species.reason: The applied species is poplar in the FOX transgenic system.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods specify Populus tremula × Populus alba.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper cites literature that FLA knockdown decreases cellulose and lignin xylem cell wall composition, but this study's own mutant line 3F90-4 is only listed with significant C5 (cellulose), not lignin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Cellulose is a polysaccharide and lignin is a phenylpropanoid polymer; both are standard and fit the paper's terminology of major cell wall constituents.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper explicitly treats cellulose and lignin as major secondary cell wall components contributing to wood formation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The study centers on xylem/wood cell wall composition, so xylem secondary cell wall is directly inferable.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Table 2 annotates the gene as FASCICLIN-LIKE ARABINOGALACTAN PROTEIN 17 (FLA17).

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure 3 indicates most recovered genes are highest in xylem, but FLA17 itself is not individually described in text.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: FLA17 corresponds to FOX line 3F90-4, and inserted FLcDNAs in FOX lines are described as highly overexpressed under the FOX system.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The FOX vector uses the CaMV 35S promoter.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Table 1 shows 3F90-4 had significant C5 change; Discussion cites FLA gene knockdown literature linking FLA genes to decreased cellulose and lignin composition.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: This line was part of the FOX screen with insert sequencing, trait profiling, and general qRT-PCR validation of inserted FLcDNA overexpression.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Plants were screened under greenhouse growth conditions.

Omics_Data.reason: Results use published RNA-seq data from Shi et al. 2017 without giving accession numbers.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper does not explicitly state FLA17 is a target for engineering, though it is a reasonable implication.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper supports that this is suggestive rather than mechanistically definitive; no direct recapitulation was done for FLA17.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points reflect the paper: FLA17-homolog was recovered in a cell wall-related mutant, authors connect FLA genes to cellulose/lignin deposition from literature, and this supports xylem-biased discovery.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Table 2 lists 1F2-4.1 as Potri.003G173300, annotated as MAP65-8.

Source_Species.reason: Source library came from an aspen tree/Populus tremuloides, not explicitly from P. tremula × P. alba.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: P. tremula × P. alba is the recipient genotype, whereas source library species is given as aspen/Populus tremuloides.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods state Populus tremula × Populus alba.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Discussion states MAP65 genes organize microtubule scaffolds associated to cellulose assembly; cellulose is the linked final wall product.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Cellulose is a polysaccharide secondary wall component.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper frames cellulose as a major structural component of secondary cell walls in wood formation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Given the xylem/wood formation focus and cell wall composition measurements, xylem secondary cell wall is directly inferable.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Table 2 names it MICROTUBULE ASSOCIATED PROTEIN 65-8 (MAP65-8).

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Microtubule association implies cytoskeletal localization, but the paper does not explicitly state 'cytoskeleton' as localization.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state most recovered genes are highest in xylem, but do not specifically single out MAP65-8.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: MAP65-8 is the insert in line 1F2-4.1, and inserted FLcDNAs are broadly shown to be highly overexpressed in FOX lines.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The FOX system used the CaMV 35S promoter.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Table 1 shows line 1F2-4 had significant C6 and lignin at P<0.01 and C5 at P<0.05; Discussion connects MAP65 genes to cellulose assembly.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by FOX screening, FLcDNA sequencing, qRT-PCR characterization of inserts, and wood chemistry trait profiling.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Greenhouse growth conditions are explicitly described.

Omics_Data.reason: Published RNA-seq data from Shi et al. 2017 were used; no accession numbers were provided in the text.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: MAP65 is microtubule-associated, but the field 'Interacting_Proteins: Microtubules' is not explicitly presented as a protein interaction result in this paper.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable application but not directly stated for MAP65-8 in the paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed presents MAP65 as a wall-related gene via microtubule organization rather than as a catalytic enzyme or TF.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All summary statements are consistent with Table 1, Table 2, and the Discussion.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Table 2 lists 3F16-6 as Potri.009G010800 annotated as LONELY GUY 1 (LOG1).

Source_Species.reason: The inserted FLcDNA came from the xylem cDNA library sourced from an aspen tree/Populus tremuloides, while 'Pta' refers to the transformed hybrid background.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source library species is not stated as P. tremula × P. alba; the recipient genotype is P. tremula × P. alba.

Applied_Species.reason: The gene was tested in transgenic poplar.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods specify Populus tremula × Populus alba as the transformation background.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results specifically measured increased active cytokinin isopentenyl adenine (iP) in 3F16-6.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: iP is explicitly presented as an active cytokinin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results and Discussion connect elevated iP with increased xylem proliferation and secondary growth.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Figure 4k,l and text state active CK iP was significantly higher in both stem and leaf tissues.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Summary and Results state LOG1 encodes an enzyme in cytokinin biosynthesis; Figure 4f depicts cytokinin activation/biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Figure 4f and text specifically discuss the iP-type cytokinin pathway involving iPRMP and iP.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper identifies the gene as LONELY GUY 1 and describes LOG genes as cytokinin riboside 5'-monophosphate phosphoribohydrolases.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number appears in the paper text.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper repeatedly refers to LOG genes as a gene family; phosphoribohydrolase activity is explicitly described.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Figure 4f and text identify iPRMP as the inactive cytokinin precursor converted by LOG.

Primary_Product.reason: Figure 4f and text identify iP as the active product.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states LOG genes directly convert iPRMP to iP in a single step.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Since LOG directly forms the active iP from iPRMP, calling it the terminal activation step is directly inferable from Figure 4f.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper explicitly states direct conversion of precursor to active cytokinin.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports a key activation/tissue-specific control role, but does not establish rate-limiting behavior.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state most recovered genes are highest in xylem, and for LOG genes specifically the majority are expressed in wood-forming tissues; Discussion states poplar LOG1 has highest expression in xylem tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: 3F16-6 overexpresses poplar LOG1 under 35S; native homolog expression in xylem/wood-forming tissues is described in Results and Discussion.

Applied_Promoters.reason: FOX construct and recapitulation overexpression used the 35S promoter.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show increased iP in stem and leaf, near doubling of xylem proliferation, increased stem diameter, and Discussion notes decreased height under ubiquitous overexpression.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are directly reported: FOX identification, qRT-PCR, cytokinin LC-MS/MS, retransformation recapitulation, and xylem width/anatomical measurements.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Plants were grown approximately 3 months in the greenhouse.

Omics_Data.reason: Results explicitly use Shi et al. 2017 and Sundell et al. 2017 expression datasets; accession numbers are not given in the main text.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Discussion explicitly states ubiquitous overexpression increased girth and decreased height growth.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion states woody biomass may be increased by such manipulations and suggests using a xylem-specific promoter to avoid pleiotropic height effects.

Other_Important_Info.reason: All elements are in the paper: 3F16-6 insert homologous to Arabidopsis and P. trichocarpa LOG1, authors refer collectively to poplar LOG1, and iPRMP decrease was significant in leaves but not stems.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported by Results and Discussion.

corrections.reason: No EC number appears in the paper text.
